mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Azure services, but it may incur additional costs compared to self-managed solutions.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure resources. This command installs the Azure CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> For Debian-based systems

steps.title: Create AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This merges the AKS cluster's credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Set Up Monitoring and Autoscaling
steps.description:
This command enables monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> This integrates Azure Monitor for containers.
> This sets up cluster autoscaling.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this action cannot be undone and will result in data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will also remove any orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workloads. This command upgrades the AKS cluster to the latest version.

```bash

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --yes

kubectl get nodes

```

> Monitor the upgrade process.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get credentials for cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource group or cluster name may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource group and cluster name with 'az aks list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Check node status and consider scaling up the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'az aks install-cli'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when accessing the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your Azure account may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the correct role assigned in Azure.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Azure regions, improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure workloads are compatible with the new version before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
